{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kamatsuka Kofun | native_name =釜塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kamatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamatsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|32|37.04|N|130|10|39|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Itoshima, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|37.04|N|130|10|39|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kamatsuka Kofun'''|釜塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Kamiari neighborhood of the city of [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162448|title=釜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamatsuka Kofun is a large {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}}-style circular tumulus built on the marshland on the east bank of the former mouth of the Nagano River in western Fukuoka Prefecture. It the stone [[burial chamber]] was excavated in 1885 and artifacts were unearthed, and three [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted since 1978. The tumulus is 56 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height, making it one of the largest circular tumuli in northern Kyushu. It was constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the southeast Cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' (including "morning glory-shaped" ''haniwa''), figurative ''haniwa'' (shield-shaped and house-shaped ''haniwa''), and ''[[fukiishi]]'' can be seen on the outside of the tumulus. A moat (5-8 meters wide) surrounds the tumulus, and an earthen bridge (crossing embankment) is located on the north side. An outer bank has been recognized on the outside of the moat, and the diameter including the outer bank is 89 meters. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial facility is a pit-type horizontal-entrance stone chamber with a box-type stone sarcophagus placed inside. The burial chamber is built by stacking flat broken stones, but large slabs of stone are erected on both sides of the entrance to form walls. There is a protrusion on the back wall for hanging something. The passage is about 100 meters long, opens in a "H" shape, and is one step higher than the main chamber. The passage is entered from above, and the stone chamber has the characteristics of an early horizontal-type stone chamber. Although [[grave goods]] are recorded as having been found when the tomb was opened in the [[Meiji period]], no details are preserved and the whereabouts of any artifacts is currently unknown. The burial chamber is currently preserved in a backfilled state.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The construction date of the tumulus is estimated to be around the first half of the 5th century, during the middle of the Kofun period. The style of the burial chamber indicates a connection with the [[Korean Peninsula]], whereas the presence of a moat also indicates a connection with the [[Yamato kingdom]]. In addition, it is unusual for a large tomb to be built on low, marshy land.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is approximately a five-minute walk from [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhi Line]] [[Kafuri Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Kamatsuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/110/080/kamatsuka-kohun.html Itoshima City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kamatsuka Kofun | native_name =釜塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kamatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamatsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|32|37.04|N|130|10|39|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Itoshima, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|37.04|N|130|10|39|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kamatsuka Kofun'''|釜塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Kamiari neighborhood of the city of [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162448|title=釜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamatsuka Kofun is a large {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus built on the marshland on the east bank of the former mouth of the Nagano River in western Fukuoka Prefecture. It the stone [[burial chamber]] was excavated in 1885 and artifacts were unearthed, and three [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted since 1978. The tumulus is 56 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height, making it one of the largest circular tumuli in northern Kyushu. It was constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the southeast Cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' (including "morning glory-shaped" ''haniwa''), figurative ''haniwa'' (shield-shaped and house-shaped ''haniwa''), and ''[[fukiishi]]'' can be seen on the outside of the tumulus. A moat (5-8 meters wide) surrounds the tumulus, and an earthen bridge (crossing embankment) is located on the north side. An outer bank has been recognized on the outside of the moat, and the diameter including the outer bank is 89 meters. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial facility is a pit-type horizontal-entrance stone chamber with a box-type stone sarcophagus placed inside. The burial chamber is built by stacking flat broken stones, but large slabs of stone are erected on both sides of the entrance to form walls. There is a protrusion on the back wall for hanging something. The passage is about 100 meters long, opens in a "H" shape, and is one step higher than the main chamber. The passage is entered from above, and the stone chamber has the characteristics of an early horizontal-type stone chamber. Although [[grave goods]] are recorded as having been found when the tomb was opened in the [[Meiji period]], no details are preserved and the whereabouts of any artifacts is currently unknown. The burial chamber is currently preserved in a backfilled state.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The construction date of the tumulus is estimated to be around the first half of the 5th century, during the middle of the Kofun period. The style of the burial chamber indicates a connection with the [[Korean Peninsula]], whereas the presence of a moat also indicates a connection with the [[Yamato kingdom]]. In addition, it is unusual for a large tomb to be built on low, marshy land.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is approximately a five-minute walk from [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhi Line]] [[Kafuri Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Kamatsuka Kofun | native_name =釜塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kamatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamatsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|32|37.04|N|130|10|39|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Itoshima, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|37.04|N|130|10|39|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kamatsuka Kofun'''|釜塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamiari neighborhood of the city of [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162448|title=釜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamatsuka Kofun is a large {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus built on the marshland on the east bank of the former mouth of the Nagano River in western Fukuoka Prefecture. It the stone [[burial chamber]] was excavated in 1885 and artifacts were unearthed, and three [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted since 1978. The tumulus is 56 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height, making it one of the largest circular tumuli in northern Kyushu. It was constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the southeast Cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' (including "morning glory-shaped" ''haniwa''), figurative ''haniwa'' (shield-shaped and house-shaped ''haniwa''), and ''[[fukiishi]]'' can be seen on the outside of the tumulus. A moat (5-8 meters wide) surrounds the tumulus, and an earthen bridge (crossing embankment) is located on the north side. An outer bank has been recognized on the outside of the moat, and the diameter including the outer bank is 89 meters. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial facility is a pit-type horizontal-entrance stone chamber with a box-type stone sarcophagus placed inside. The burial chamber is built by stacking flat broken stones, but large slabs of stone are erected on both sides of the entrance to form walls. There is a protrusion on the back wall for hanging something. The passage is about 100 meters long, opens in a "H" shape, and is one step higher than the main chamber. The passage is entered from above, and the stone chamber has the characteristics of an early horizontal-type stone chamber. Although [[grave goods]] are recorded as having been found when the tomb was opened in the [[Meiji period]], no details are preserved and the whereabouts of any artifacts is currently unknown. The burial chamber is currently preserved in a backfilled state.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The construction date of the tumulus is estimated to be around the first half of the 5th century, during the middle[[Category:middle]] of the Kofun period. The style of the burial chamber indicates a connection with the [[Korean Peninsula]], whereas the presence of a moat also indicates a connection with the [[Yamato kingdom]]. In addition, it is unusual for a large tomb to be built on low, marshy land.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is approximately a five-minute walk from [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhi Line]] [[Kafuri Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:11]][[Category:-inline|Kamatsuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/110/080/kamatsuka-kohun.html Itoshima City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | name = Kamatsuka Kofun | native_name =釜塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kamatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamatsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|32|37.04|N|130|10|39|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Itoshima, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|37.04|N|130|10|39|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kamatsuka Kofun'''|釜塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamiari neighborhood of the city of [[Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162448|title=釜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamatsuka Kofun is a large {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus built on the marshland on the east bank of the former mouth of the Nagano River in western Fukuoka Prefecture. It the stone [[burial chamber]] was excavated in 1885 and artifacts were unearthed, and three [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted since 1978. The tumulus is 56 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height, making it one of the largest circular tumuli in northern Kyushu. It was constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the southeast Cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' (including "morning glory-shaped" ''haniwa''), figurative ''haniwa'' (shield-shaped and house-shaped ''haniwa''), and ''[[fukiishi]]'' can be seen on the outside of the tumulus. A moat (5-8 meters wide) surrounds the tumulus, and an earthen bridge (crossing embankment) is located on the north side. An outer bank has been recognized on the outside of the moat, and the diameter including the outer bank is 89 meters. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial facility is a pit-type horizontal-entrance stone chamber with a box-type stone sarcophagus placed inside. The burial chamber is built by stacking flat broken stones, but large slabs of stone are erected on both sides of the entrance to form walls. There is a protrusion on the back wall for hanging something. The passage is about 100 meters long, opens in a "H" shape, and is one step higher than the main chamber. The passage is entered from above, and the stone chamber has the characteristics of an early horizontal-type stone chamber. Although [[grave goods]] are recorded as having been found when the tomb was opened in the [[Meiji period]], no details are preserved and the whereabouts of any artifacts is currently unknown. The burial chamber is currently preserved in a backfilled state.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The construction date of the tumulus is estimated to be around the first half of the 5th century, during the middleof the Kofun period. The style of the burial chamber indicates a connection with the [[Korean Peninsula]], whereas the presence of a moat also indicates a connection with the [[Yamato kingdom]]. In addition, it is unusual for a large tomb to be built on low, marshy land.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is approximately a five-minute walk from [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhi Line]] [[Kafuri Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Kamatsuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/110/080/kamatsuka-kohun.html Itoshima City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Kamatsuka Kofun'''|釜塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamiari neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162448|title=釜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamatsuka Kofun is a large {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus built on the marshland on the east bank of the former mouth of the Nagano River in western Fukuoka Prefecture. It the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was excavated in 1885 and artifacts were unearthed, and three [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted since 1978. The tumulus is 56 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height, making it one of the largest circular tumuli in northern Kyushu. It was constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the southeast Cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' (including "morning glory-shaped" ''haniwa''), figurative ''haniwa'' (shield-shaped and house-shaped ''haniwa''), and ''[[fukiishi]]'' can be seen on the outside of the tumulus. A moat (5-8 meters wide) surrounds the tumulus, and an earthen bridge (crossing embankment) is located on the north side. An outer bank has been recognized on the outside of the moat, and the diameter including the outer bank is 89 meters. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial facility is a pit-type horizontal-entrance stone chamber with a box-type stone sarcophagus placed inside. The burial chamber is built by stacking flat broken stones, but large slabs of stone are erected on both sides of the entrance to form walls. There is a protrusion on the back wall for hanging something. The passage is about 100 meters long, opens in a "H" shape, and is one step higher than the main chamber. The passage is entered from above, and the stone chamber has the characteristics of an early horizontal-type stone chamber. Although [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] are recorded as having been found when the tomb was opened in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], no details are preserved and the whereabouts of any artifacts is currently unknown. The burial chamber is currently preserved in a backfilled state.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The construction date of the tumulus is estimated to be around the first half of the 5th century, during the middleof the Kofun period. The style of the burial chamber indicates a connection with the [[:en:Korea|Korea]], whereas the presence of a moat also indicates a connection with the [[:en:Yamato kingdom|Yamato kingdom]]. In addition, it is unusual for a large tomb to be built on low, marshy land.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is approximately a five-minute walk from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] [[:en:Kafuri Station|Kafuri Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kamatsuka Kofun]][[en:Kamatsuka Kofun]][[ja:釜塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q48759963]] Wikidata name: Kamatsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q48759963]] Wikidata name: Kamatsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kamatsuka Kofun | native_name =釜塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kamatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamatsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.543622222222|N|130.17770555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|37.04|N|130|10|39|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kamatsuka Kofun'''|釜塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamiari neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162448|title=釜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamatsuka Kofun is a large {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus built on the marshland on the east bank of the former mouth of the Nagano River in western Fukuoka Prefecture. It the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was excavated in 1885 and artifacts were unearthed, and three [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted since 1978. The tumulus is 56 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height, making it one of the largest circular tumuli in northern Kyushu. It was constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the southeast Cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' (including "morning glory-shaped" ''haniwa''), figurative ''haniwa'' (shield-shaped and house-shaped ''haniwa''), and ''[[fukiishi]]'' can be seen on the outside of the tumulus. A moat (5-8 meters wide) surrounds the tumulus, and an earthen bridge (crossing embankment) is located on the north side. An outer bank has been recognized on the outside of the moat, and the diameter including the outer bank is 89 meters. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial facility is a pit-type horizontal-entrance stone chamber with a box-type stone sarcophagus placed inside. The burial chamber is built by stacking flat broken stones, but large slabs of stone are erected on both sides of the entrance to form walls. There is a protrusion on the back wall for hanging something. The passage is about 100 meters long, opens in a "H" shape, and is one step higher than the main chamber. The passage is entered from above, and the stone chamber has the characteristics of an early horizontal-type stone chamber. Although [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] are recorded as having been found when the tomb was opened in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], no details are preserved and the whereabouts of any artifacts is currently unknown. The burial chamber is currently preserved in a backfilled state.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The construction date of the tumulus is estimated to be around the first half of the 5th century, during the middleof the Kofun period. The style of the burial chamber indicates a connection with the [[:en:Korea|Korea]], whereas the presence of a moat also indicates a connection with the [[:en:Yamato kingdom|Yamato kingdom]]. In addition, it is unusual for a large tomb to be built on low, marshy land.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is approximately a five-minute walk from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] [[:en:Kafuri Station|Kafuri Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Kamatsuka Kofun'''|釜塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamiari neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162448|title=釜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamatsuka Kofun is a large {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus built on the marshland on the east bank of the former mouth of the Nagano River in western Fukuoka Prefecture. It the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was excavated in 1885 and artifacts were unearthed, and three [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted since 1978. The tumulus is 56 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height, making it one of the largest circular tumuli in northern Kyushu. It was constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the southeast Cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' (including "morning glory-shaped" ''haniwa''), figurative ''haniwa'' (shield-shaped and house-shaped ''haniwa''), and ''[[fukiishi]]'' can be seen on the outside of the tumulus. A moat (5-8 meters wide) surrounds the tumulus, and an earthen bridge (crossing embankment) is located on the north side. An outer bank has been recognized on the outside of the moat, and the diameter including the outer bank is 89 meters. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial facility is a pit-type horizontal-entrance stone chamber with a box-type stone sarcophagus placed inside. The burial chamber is built by stacking flat broken stones, but large slabs of stone are erected on both sides of the entrance to form walls. There is a protrusion on the back wall for hanging something. The passage is about 100 meters long, opens in a "H" shape, and is one step higher than the main chamber. The passage is entered from above, and the stone chamber has the characteristics of an early horizontal-type stone chamber. Although [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] are recorded as having been found when the tomb was opened in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], no details are preserved and the whereabouts of any artifacts is currently unknown. The burial chamber is currently preserved in a backfilled state.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The construction date of the tumulus is estimated to be around the first half of the 5th century, during the middleof the Kofun period. The style of the burial chamber indicates a connection with the [[:en:Korea|Korea]], whereas the presence of a moat also indicates a connection with the [[:en:Yamato kingdom|Yamato kingdom]]. In addition, it is unusual for a large tomb to be built on low, marshy land.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is approximately a five-minute walk from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] [[:en:Kafuri Station|Kafuri Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Kamatsuka Kofun'''|釜塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamiari neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162448|title=釜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamatsuka Kofun is a large {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus built on the marshland on the east bank of the former mouth of the Nagano River in western Fukuoka Prefecture. It the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was excavated in 1885 and artifacts were unearthed, and three [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted since 1978. The tumulus is 56 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height, making it one of the largest circular tumuli in northern Kyushu. It was constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the southeast Cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' (including "morning glory-shaped" ''haniwa''), figurative ''haniwa'' (shield-shaped and house-shaped ''haniwa''), and ''[[fukiishi]]'' can be seen on the outside of the tumulus. A moat (5-8 meters wide) surrounds the tumulus, and an earthen bridge (crossing embankment) is located on the north side. An outer bank has been recognized on the outside of the moat, and the diameter including the outer bank is 89 meters. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial facility is a pit-type horizontal-entrance stone chamber with a box-type stone sarcophagus placed inside. The burial chamber is built by stacking flat broken stones, but large slabs of stone are erected on both sides of the entrance to form walls. There is a protrusion on the back wall for hanging something. The passage is about 100 meters long, opens in a "H" shape, and is one step higher than the main chamber. The passage is entered from above, and the stone chamber has the characteristics of an early horizontal-type stone chamber. Although [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] are recorded as having been found when the tomb was opened in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], no details are preserved and the whereabouts of any artifacts is currently unknown. The burial chamber is currently preserved in a backfilled state.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The construction date of the tumulus is estimated to be around the first half of the 5th century, during the middleof the Kofun period. The style of the burial chamber indicates a connection with the [[:en:Korea|Korea]], whereas the presence of a moat also indicates a connection with the [[:en:Yamato kingdom|Yamato kingdom]]. In addition, it is unusual for a large tomb to be built on low, marshy land.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is approximately a five-minute walk from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] [[:en:Kafuri Station|Kafuri Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus is approximately a five-minute walk from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] [[:en:Kafuri Station|Kafuri Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Kamatsuka Kofun'''|釜塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamiari neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162448|title=釜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamatsuka Kofun is a large {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus built on the marshland on the east bank of the former mouth of the Nagano River in western Fukuoka Prefecture. It the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was excavated in 1885 and artifacts were unearthed, and three [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted since 1978. The tumulus is 56 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height, making it one of the largest circular tumuli in northern Kyushu. It was constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the southeast Cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' (including "morning glory-shaped" ''haniwa''), figurative ''haniwa'' (shield-shaped and house-shaped ''haniwa''), and ''[[fukiishi]]'' can be seen on the outside of the tumulus. A moat (5-8 meters wide) surrounds the tumulus, and an earthen bridge (crossing embankment) is located on the north side. An outer bank has been recognized on the outside of the moat, and the diameter including the outer bank is 89 meters. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial facility is a pit-type horizontal-entrance stone chamber with a box-type stone sarcophagus placed inside. The burial chamber is built by stacking flat broken stones, but large slabs of stone are erected on both sides of the entrance to form walls. There is a protrusion on the back wall for hanging something. The passage is about 100 meters long, opens in a "H" shape, and is one step higher than the main chamber. The passage is entered from above, and the stone chamber has the characteristics of an early horizontal-type stone chamber. Although [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] are recorded as having been found when the tomb was opened in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], no details are preserved and the whereabouts of any artifacts is currently unknown. The burial chamber is currently preserved in a backfilled state.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The construction date of the tumulus is estimated to be around the first half of the 5th century, during the middleof the Kofun period. The style of the burial chamber indicates a connection with the [[:en:Korea|Korea]], whereas the presence of a moat also indicates a connection with the [[:en:Yamato kingdom|Yamato kingdom]]. In addition, it is unusual for a large tomb to be built on low, marshy land.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is approximately a five-minute walk from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] [[:en:Kafuri Station|Kafuri Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

An outer bank has been recognized on the outside of the moat, and the diameter including the outer bank is 89 meters. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial facility is a pit-type horizontal-entrance stone chamber with a box-type stone sarcophagus placed inside. The burial chamber is built by stacking flat broken stones, but large slabs of stone are erected on both sides of the entrance to form walls. There is a protrusion on the back wall for hanging something. The passage is about 100 meters long, opens in a "H" shape, and is one step higher than the main chamber. The passage is entered from above, and the stone chamber has the characteristics of an early horizontal-type stone chamber. Although [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] are recorded as having been found when the tomb was opened in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], no details are preserved and the whereabouts of any artifacts is currently unknown. The burial chamber is currently preserved in a backfilled state.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The construction date of the tumulus is estimated to be around the first half of the 5th century, during the middleof the Kofun period. The style of the burial chamber indicates a connection with the [[:en:Korea|Korea]], whereas the presence of a moat also indicates a connection with the [[:en:Yamato kingdom|Yamato kingdom]]. In addition, it is unusual for a large tomb to be built on low, marshy land.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is approximately a five-minute walk from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] [[:en:Kafuri Station|Kafuri Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|37.04|N|130|10|39|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kamatsuka Kofun'''|釜塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamiari neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en|Itoshima, Fukuoka|WD=Q1011145|lt=Itoshima}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162448|title=釜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamatsuka Kofun is a large {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus built on the marshland on the east bank of the former mouth of the Nagano River in western Fukuoka Prefecture. It the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was excavated in 1885 and artifacts were unearthed, and three [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted since 1978. The tumulus is 56 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height, making it one of the largest circular tumuli in northern Kyushu. It was constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the southeast Cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' (including "morning glory-shaped" ''haniwa''), figurative ''haniwa'' (shield-shaped and house-shaped ''haniwa''), and ''[[fukiishi]]'' can be seen on the outside of the tumulus. A moat (5-8 meters wide) surrounds the tumulus, and an earthen bridge (crossing embankment) is located on the north side. An outer bank has been recognized on the outside of the moat, and the diameter including the outer bank is 89 meters. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial facility is a pit-type horizontal-entrance stone chamber with a box-type stone sarcophagus placed inside. The burial chamber is built by stacking flat broken stones, but large slabs of stone are erected on both sides of the entrance to form walls. There is a protrusion on the back wall for hanging something. The passage is about 100 meters long, opens in a "H" shape, and is one step higher than the main chamber. The passage is entered from above, and the stone chamber has the characteristics of an early horizontal-type stone chamber. Although {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} are recorded as having been found when the tomb was opened in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, no details are preserved and the whereabouts of any artifacts is currently unknown. The burial chamber is currently preserved in a backfilled state.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The construction date of the tumulus is estimated to be around the first half of the 5th century, during the middleof the Kofun period. The style of the burial chamber indicates a connection with the {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|WD=Q18097|lt=Korea}}, whereas the presence of a moat also indicates a connection with the [[:en:Yamato kingdom|Yamato kingdom]]. In addition, it is unusual for a large tomb to be built on low, marshy land.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is approximately a five-minute walk from {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Chikuhi Line|en|Chikuhi Line|WD=Q1042132|lt=Chikuhi Line}} {{ill|Kafuri Station|en|Kafuri Station|WD=Q6346295|lt=Kafuri Station}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kamatsuka Kofun | native_name =釜塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kamatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamatsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.543622222222|N|130.17770555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en|Itoshima, Fukuoka|WD=Q1011145|lt=Itoshima, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|37.04|N|130|10|39|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kamatsuka Kofun'''|釜塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamiari neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en|Itoshima, Fukuoka|WD=Q1011145|lt=Itoshima}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162448|title=釜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamatsuka Kofun is a large {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus built on the marshland on the east bank of the former mouth of the Nagano River in western Fukuoka Prefecture. It the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was excavated in 1885 and artifacts were unearthed, and three [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted since 1978. The tumulus is 56 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height, making it one of the largest circular tumuli in northern Kyushu. It was constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the southeast Cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' (including "morning glory-shaped" ''haniwa''), figurative ''haniwa'' (shield-shaped and house-shaped ''haniwa''), and ''[[fukiishi]]'' can be seen on the outside of the tumulus. A moat (5-8 meters wide) surrounds the tumulus, and an earthen bridge (crossing embankment) is located on the north side. An outer bank has been recognized on the outside of the moat, and the diameter including the outer bank is 89 meters. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial facility is a pit-type horizontal-entrance stone chamber with a box-type stone sarcophagus placed inside. The burial chamber is built by stacking flat broken stones, but large slabs of stone are erected on both sides of the entrance to form walls. There is a protrusion on the back wall for hanging something. The passage is about 100 meters long, opens in a "H" shape, and is one step higher than the main chamber. The passage is entered from above, and the stone chamber has the characteristics of an early horizontal-type stone chamber. Although {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} are recorded as having been found when the tomb was opened in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, no details are preserved and the whereabouts of any artifacts is currently unknown. The burial chamber is currently preserved in a backfilled state.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The construction date of the tumulus is estimated to be around the first half of the 5th century, during the middleof the Kofun period. The style of the burial chamber indicates a connection with the {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|WD=Q18097|lt=Korea}}, whereas the presence of a moat also indicates a connection with the [[:en:Yamato kingdom|Yamato kingdom]]. In addition, it is unusual for a large tomb to be built on low, marshy land.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is approximately a five-minute walk from {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Chikuhi Line|en|Chikuhi Line|WD=Q1042132|lt=Chikuhi Line}} {{ill|Kafuri Station|en|Kafuri Station|WD=Q6346295|lt=Kafuri Station}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}}

An outer bank has been recognized on the outside of the moat, and the diameter including the outer bank is 89 meters. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial facility is a pit-type horizontal-entrance stone chamber with a box-type stone sarcophagus placed inside. The burial chamber is built by stacking flat broken stones, but large slabs of stone are erected on both sides of the entrance to form walls. There is a protrusion on the back wall for hanging something. The passage is about 100 meters long, opens in a "H" shape, and is one step higher than the main chamber. The passage is entered from above, and the stone chamber has the characteristics of an early horizontal-type stone chamber. Although {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} are recorded as having been found when the tomb was opened in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, no details are preserved and the whereabouts of any artifacts is currently unknown. The burial chamber is currently preserved in a backfilled state.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The construction date of the tumulus is estimated to be around the first half of the 5th century, during the middleof the Kofun period. The style of the burial chamber indicates a connection with the {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|WD=Q18097|lt=Korea|1=Korea}}, whereas the presence of a moat also indicates a connection with the [[:en:Yamato kingdom|Yamato kingdom]]. In addition, it is unusual for a large tomb to be built on low, marshy land.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kamatsuka Kofun | native_name =釜塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kamatsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamatsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.543622222222|N|130.17770555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en|Itoshima, Fukuoka|WD=Q1011145|lt=Itoshima, Fukuoka|1=Itoshima}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|32|37.04|N|130|10|39|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kamatsuka Kofun'''|釜塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamiari neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en|Itoshima, Fukuoka|WD=Q1011145|lt=Itoshima|1=Itoshima}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162448|title=釜塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamatsuka Kofun is a large {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus built on the marshland on the east bank of the former mouth of the Nagano River in western Fukuoka Prefecture. It the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was excavated in 1885 and artifacts were unearthed, and three [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted since 1978. The tumulus is 56 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height, making it one of the largest circular tumuli in northern Kyushu. It was constructed in three tiers and is orientated to the southeast Cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' (including "morning glory-shaped" ''haniwa''), figurative ''haniwa'' (shield-shaped and house-shaped ''haniwa''), and ''[[fukiishi]]'' can be seen on the outside of the tumulus. A moat (5-8 meters wide) surrounds the tumulus, and an earthen bridge (crossing embankment) is located on the north side. An outer bank has been recognized on the outside of the moat, and the diameter including the outer bank is 89 meters. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial facility is a pit-type horizontal-entrance stone chamber with a box-type stone sarcophagus placed inside. The burial chamber is built by stacking flat broken stones, but large slabs of stone are erected on both sides of the entrance to form walls. There is a protrusion on the back wall for hanging something. The passage is about 100 meters long, opens in a "H" shape, and is one step higher than the main chamber. The passage is entered from above, and the stone chamber has the characteristics of an early horizontal-type stone chamber. Although {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} are recorded as having been found when the tomb was opened in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, no details are preserved and the whereabouts of any artifacts is currently unknown. The burial chamber is currently preserved in a backfilled state.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The construction date of the tumulus is estimated to be around the first half of the 5th century, during the middleof the Kofun period. The style of the burial chamber indicates a connection with the {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|WD=Q18097|lt=Korea|1=Korea}}, whereas the presence of a moat also indicates a connection with the [[:en:Yamato kingdom|Yamato kingdom]]. In addition, it is unusual for a large tomb to be built on low, marshy land.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is approximately a five-minute walk from {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Chikuhi Line|en|Chikuhi Line|WD=Q1042132|lt=Chikuhi Line|1=Chikuhi Line}} {{ill|Kafuri Station|en|Kafuri Station|WD=Q6346295|lt=Kafuri Station|1=Kafuri Station}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Kamatsuka Kofun}} *[https://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/110/080/kamatsuka-kohun.html Itoshima City home page] {{in lang|ja}}
